title: wollastonia biflora
intro: Wollastonia biflora, also known as the forked leaf or two-flowered Wollastonia, is a plant species native to Australia, commonly found in dry forests and woodlands. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly in traditional Aboriginal medicine, where it is used to treat various ailments, including fever, sore throats, and skin conditions. Wollastonia biflora has been found to have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which contribute to its health benefits, such as reducing inflammation and promoting wound healing. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and extracts, making it a popular ingredient in various traditional and modern herbal remedies.
